summary: Video Conference Attendance Evidence Engine - All 17 backend tests PASSED, frontend UI verified. Conservative rules working correctly: Google Meet always returns low confidence and manual_review, Zoom/Teams with email match return high confidence and auto-decision.
passed_tests: POST /api/video-evidence/{apt_id}/ingest — Zoom with exact email match → high confidence, joined_on_time ✓
POST /api/video-evidence/{apt_id}/ingest — Teams with exact email match → high confidence ✓
POST /api/video-evidence/{apt_id}/ingest — Google Meet → ALWAYS low confidence, ALWAYS manual_review ✓
POST /api/video-evidence/{apt_id}/ingest — Ambiguous logs (name-only match) → low confidence ✓
POST /api/video-evidence/{apt_id}/ingest — No matching participant → returns unmatched ✓
POST /api/video-evidence/{apt_id}/ingest — Duplicate payload → returns error about already ingested ✓
POST /api/video-evidence/{apt_id}/ingest — Invalid JSON payload → returns error ✓
GET /api/video-evidence/{apt_id} — Returns video evidence for appointment ✓
POST /api/attendance/evaluate/{apt_id} — Zoom with video evidence → on_time (auto-decision) ✓
POST /api/attendance/evaluate/{apt_id} — Teams with video evidence → on_time (auto-decision) ✓
POST /api/attendance/evaluate/{apt_id} — Meet with video evidence → manual_review (no auto-penalty) ✓
POST /api/attendance/evaluate/{apt_id} — Physical appointment still works correctly (no regression) ✓
Frontend: Video appointment detail shows provider badge (Zoom/Teams/Meet) ✓
Frontend: Video evidence section shows timeline with confidence badges, join/leave times ✓
Frontend: Ingestion form allows selecting provider, entering JSON, submitting ✓
Frontend: Ingestion logs visible in appointment detail (Historique d'ingestion) ✓
critical_code_review_comments: MeetAdapter correctly caps identity_confidence at 'low' regardless of email match (line 72-82 in meet_adapter.py)
ZoomAdapter and TeamsAdapter have EVIDENCE_CEILING = 'strong' for auto-decisions
attendance_service.py correctly handles video_provider_ceiling == 'assisted' → always manual_review (line 134-147)
video_evidence_service.py correctly computes video_outcome = 'manual_review' for assisted ceiling (line 329-330)
Frontend AppointmentDetail.js correctly displays provider badges with color coding (getProviderIcon function)
Frontend shows 'Confiance faible' for Meet and 'Confiance haute' for Zoom/Teams
seed_data_creation: Used existing test appointments with video evidence already ingested
context_for_next_testing_agent: Video evidence system fully tested. Key conservative rules verified: 1) Google Meet evidence is ALWAYS low confidence and results in manual_review, 2) Zoom/Teams with email match get high confidence and auto-decision, 3) Name-only matches get low confidence. Physical appointment logic unchanged. Frontend shows correct provider badges and confidence levels.

video_evidence_ui_elements_verified.provider_badge: Shows Zoom/Teams/Google Meet with color coding
video_evidence_ui_elements_verified.ingestion_form: Provider dropdown, external ID, URL, JSON textarea, submit button
video_evidence_ui_elements_verified.ingestion_logs: Historique d'ingestion shows all past ingestions with timestamps
